brief.headline_zh: 欧洲SEP司法与监管双重变局
brief.headline_en: EU SEP Landscape: UK Global FRAND Authority, UPC Anti-Suit Injunctions, and Regulatory Shifts
brief.summary_zh: 2025-2026年，英国法院确立全球FRAND费率裁定权并倾向仲裁，UPC强化反禁诉令及禁令效力。UKIPO启动SEP改革以解决许可不透明问题。NPE利用新平台加剧诉讼，中美欧管辖权冲突激化。中国车企加速加入专利池，但面临更高合规成本与跨境诉讼风险，需重构许可策略。
key_points: 英国司法确立全球FRAND定价权威并转向支持仲裁，削弱传统诉讼威慑力
UPC通过反禁诉令(AASI)巩固管辖权，NPE利用程序规则发起密集攻势
UKIPO推进监管改革，叠加美欧反垄断分歧，许可环境不确定性增加
actions: 建立“许可意愿”快速响应机制，收到通知即刻书面表态以防丧失抗辩权
优先选择Avanci/Via等专利池获取一揽子许可，规避分散诉讼的高昂成本
针对英国高院裁决调整交叉许可谈判底线，预留应对AASI的预案
watchlist: UKIPO 2026年SEP改革细则落地及其对现有协议的影响
英国最高法院在特斯拉等案中关于全球费率管辖权的最终立场
中美法院在FRAND费率认定上的冲突升级及执行互认风险

sep_ai_analysis.summary_zh: SEP 长窗从 2026.03 累计到现在，共识别 91 条高相关信号。近120天里，许可动作占比最高，欧洲层面仍是最活跃场域。
sep_ai_analysis.summary_en: The SEP long window now spans from Mar 2026 to the present with 91 high-relevance signals. In the recent 120-day window, Licensing lead the mix and Europe-wide remains the busiest forum.

insight_cards.insight_zh: 许可动作已经从背景变量变成当前主线，说明专利池扩围、费率博弈和双边谈判正在持续抬高经营准备门槛。
insight_cards.insight_en: Licensing has moved from a background factor to the main storyline, showing pools, rate-setting, and bilateral talks are raising the preparation bar.
insight_cards.action_zh: 优先把许可覆盖、费率对标和 willing licensee 证据准备前置。
insight_cards.action_en: Front-load licence coverage checks, rate benchmarking, and willing-licensee evidence.
insight_cards.evidence_zh: 近120天 许可动作 59 / 前120天 0 / 长窗累计 59

insight_cards.insight_zh: 欧洲层面是近120天最活跃的场域，英国紧随其后，争议正在形成多法域联动。
insight_cards.insight_en: Europe-wide is the busiest forum in the recent window, with United Kingdom close behind, pointing to multi-forum spillover.
insight_cards.action_zh: 优先盯住 欧洲层面 的裁判与程序变化，并同步观察外溢到其他法域的连锁影响。
insight_cards.action_en: Track procedural and decisional shifts in Europe-wide first, then watch for spillover into other venues.

insight_cards.insight_zh: 近120天中企相关信号共有 30 条，占近期样本 33%。重点企业包括 ZTE / Huawei / Honor。
insight_cards.insight_en: There are 30 China-related signals in the recent 120-day window, or 33% of the sample. Focus companies include ZTE / Huawei / Honor.
insight_cards.action_zh: 把许可准备、诉讼预案和客户交付协同放到同一张经营清单里，不要只按单案应对。
insight_cards.action_en: Put licensing readiness, litigation planning, and customer-delivery coordination on the same operating checklist rather than treating them as one-off disputes.

insight_cards.insight_zh: 当前约 100% 的专题样本基于全文或公开预览分析，摘要级样本主要用于补趋势，不宜单独放大解读。

insight_cards.action_zh: 对涉及重大经营判断的节点，优先回到原文或公开预览核实，再决定是否升级为重点预警。

brief.china_impact_summary_zh: 这条长窗显示，SEP 风险已经从传统通信终端延伸到车载与 IoT，并在德国、英国和 UPC 三条诉讼线同步抬升。对中国企业来说，下一阶段重点不只是单案应对，而是把许可准备、诉讼预案和规则跟踪并行推进。
china_action_points: 涉及车载、蜂窝、Wi-Fi 或 IoT 出海业务的中国企业，应尽快核查专利池与双边许可覆盖，避免产品上市后再被动补谈。
在德国、英国和 UPC 有销售、供应链或客户落点的企业，应同步准备 FRAND 谈判材料、可比许可证据与禁令应对预案。
法务、标准与商务团队需要持续跟踪英欧 SEP 政策和竞争法变化，避免沿用单一市场的许可策略应对欧洲。
针对已进入欧洲市场的中国终端、部件与整车企业，建议把 SEP 许可预算、争议升级路径和管辖博弈纳入年度经营安排。

brief.summary_en: Between 2025 and 2026, the European standard-essential patent (SEP) landscape underwent significant judicial and regulatory transformation. The UK High Court established authority to determine global FRAND rates for cross-licenses, while the UK Court of Appeal validated arbitration-based licensing offers, shifting dispute resolution dynamics. items.title_en: UKIPO Signals Upcoming Standard Essential Patent Regulatory Reforms

items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月11日，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布2026-2027企业计划，明确将标准必要专利（SEP）作为政策重点。针对当前生态系统存在的许可不透明、必要性不确定及争议解决成本高企等结构性挑战，UKIPO计划在2026年完成政策制定并推动监管框架变革。此举旨在提升英国创新吸引力，虽无即时立法，但预示未来将在许可透明度、SEP必要性认定及纠纷效率方面进行改革。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, identifying standard essential patents as a key policy priority. The plan acknowledges longstanding structural challenges within the current ecosystem, specifically limited licensing transparency, uncertainty regarding patent essentiality, and costly dispute resolution mechanisms that disproportionately impact small and medium enterprises. While no immediate legislative changes are introduced, the UKIPO intends to complete policy development in 2026 to drive regulatory framework reforms. These initiatives aim to enhance the UK’s attractiveness as a leading jurisdiction for innovation by addressing inefficiencies and improving certainty for stakeholders involved in SEP licensing and enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信及科技企业在英面临更严格的SEP合规环境。建议密切关注UKIPO后续政策细则，提前评估现有许可协议的透明度与必要性条款风险。应加强内部SEP管理能力，积极参与行业咨询，以应对潜在的监管变化及更高的争议解决效率要求，避免在新一轮改革中处于被动地位。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders engaged in SEP licensing and enforcement should anticipate a stricter compliance environment driven by upcoming UK regulatory adjustments. It is prudent to evaluate existing licensing agreements for transparency and essentiality clause risks ahead of formal rule changes. Organizations should strengthen internal SEP management capabilities and actively participate in industry consultations to shape emerging standards. Proactive engagement will help mitigate potential liabilities and ensure readiness for more efficient, yet rigorous, dispute resolution processes expected under the new framework.

items.title: Come Together? Acer v Nokia and the Contractual Turn of FRAND Arbitration
items.title_zh: Acer诉诺基亚案：FRAND仲裁的合同化转向
items.title_en: Acer v Nokia: The Contractual Turn of FRAND Arbitration in the UK Court of Appeal
items.summary: "The readiness is all." - HamletSometimes a judgment does not change the melody of FRAND law. It changes the instrument through which that melody may be played. Not every player reads the same score, and not every performance is equally faithful to the underlying composition. What the UK Court of Appeal has done in Acer v Nokia is reassign one voice in the ensemble - without rewriting the work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Acer诉诺基亚案中裁定，标准必要专利（SEP）权利人提供的“可调整许可要约”构成客观上的RAND要约。若实施者拒绝该包含仲裁条款的要约，将丧失在英国法院寻求独立RAND费率裁定的资格。此判决未改变FRAND法律实质，但确立了通过国际商会（ICC）仲裁解决全球许可争议的合法路径，强化了合同自由在SEP纠纷中的核心地位。
items.ai_summary_en: In Acer Inc. v. Nokia Technologies Oy, the UK Court of Appeal ruled that a standard-essential patent holder's 'Adjustable Licence Offer' constitutes an objectively RAND-compliant proposal. This offer included an immediate interim license with final terms to be determined by an International Chamber of Commerce arbitral tribunal. The court held that if implementers refuse such an arbitration-linked offer, they forfeit their right to seek an independent RAND rate determination from English courts. While this judgment does not alter the substantive principles of FRAND law, it establishes arbitration as a legitimate mechanism for fulfilling FRAND obligations. Consequently, contractual freedom and alternative dispute resolution mechanisms now play a central role in resolving global SEP licensing disputes.
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly impacts SEP licensing strategies by elevating the status of arbitration within the FRAND framework. Rights holders can now leverage arbitration-linked offers to potentially block parallel court proceedings in England, shifting the venue for rate determinations. For implementers, rejecting such offers carries substantial procedural risks, including the loss of access to specific judicial forums. Practitioners must carefully evaluate the strategic implications of accepting or rejecting interim licenses tied to arbitration, as this may dictate the ultimate forum for resolving global licensing disputes and affect the balance of power in negotiations.

items.title: Patent Trolls : La Riposte Commence

items.title_en: NPEs Leverage UPC for SEP Enforcement as Patent Trolling Resurges in Europe
items.summary: Une époque révèle ses contradictions par les procès qu’elle intente. Celle-ci n’y échappe pas. Tandis que l’Europe s’efforce de bâtir un cadre juridique pour l’intelligence artificielle...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，尽管欧盟致力构建AI法律框架，但“专利流氓”（NPE）活动愈发猖獗。NPE不从事生产，仅通过收购专利组合，利用诉讼威胁迫使企业支付许可费。美国是其传统温床，而新成立的统一专利法院（UPC）正成为其新的战略论坛。文章以InterDigital起诉Disney+为例，展示NPE在多法域针对HDR等标准必要专利（SEP）发起进攻的策略。核心结论是，欧洲法律未将禁令与专利实际使用挂钩，使得NPE策略合法且高效。
items.ai_summary_en: While the European Union advances its legal framework for artificial intelligence, non-practicing entities (NPEs) are intensifying patent enforcement activities. These entities acquire patent portfolios not for industrial exploitation but to leverage litigation threats into licensing agreements. Although the United States remains a traditional hub for such practices due to favorable procedural rules, the newly established Unified Patent Court (UPC) is emerging as a strategic forum for NPEs. The article highlights InterDigital’s multi-jurisdictional lawsuit against Disney+ regarding standard-essential patents (SEPs) like HDR technology. This case illustrates how NPEs utilize cross-border litigation to exert pressure. Crucially, European law does not condition injunctive relief on the actual working of the patent, rendering these strategies legally valid and highly effective for rights holders seeking to monetize intellectual property assets through enforcement rather than production.
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating in Europe face heightened risks of SEP litigation initiated by NPEs leveraging the procedural advantages of the Unified Patent Court. It is critical to proactively assess SEP compliance and establish rapid response mechanisms for potential infringement claims. When confronted with NPE allegations, parties should actively evaluate FRAND defense arguments to avoid being coerced into excessive settlements. Furthermore, monitoring UPC jurisprudence regarding the granting of injunctions is essential, as judicial trends will significantly influence the strategic balance between patent holders and implementers in future disputes.

items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Dominant Position in FRAND and Implementer Willingness in Dolby v Beko
items.summary: The Düsseldorf Local Division held that a dominant position within the meaning of Article 102 TFEU may arise if smart TVs complying with a common standard...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方法院在 Dolby 诉 Beko 案中裁定，若符合标准的智能电视无许可无法上市，专利权人可能拥有市场支配地位。法院强调，若实施者未在 FRAND 谈判初期表明许可意愿，则无需审查专利权人报价是否合规。该判决遵循华为诉中兴案框架，并与曼海姆分院立场保持一致。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division ruled that a dominant position under Article 102 TFEU may exist if standard-compliant smart TVs cannot be marketed without a license for the patent-in-suit. The court emphasized that consumer expectations regarding common audio and video codecs are decisive. Furthermore, the judgment clarifies that if an implementer fails to demonstrate willingness to conclude a license during the initial stages of FRAND negotiations, the patentee’s offer need not be examined for compliance. This decision aligns with the Huawei v ZTE framework and maintains consistency with the Mannheim Local Division’s approach in Panasonic v Oppo, reinforcing procedural strictness in FRAND defense strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧出口智能电视等标准产品时，须在收到侵权通知后立即明确表达许可意愿，否则将丧失 FRAND 抗辩机会。建议提前建立标准化应对流程，避免因程序瑕疵导致败诉风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Implementers of standard-essential patents must explicitly express willingness to negotiate a license immediately upon receiving an infringement notice. Failure to do so at the preliminary stage precludes further review of the patentee’s licensing terms, effectively waiving FRAND defenses. Companies should establish standardized internal protocols to ensure timely, written responses to infringement claims. Proactive risk assessment and prepared negotiation strategies are essential to avoid procedural defaults that could lead to unfavorable injunctions or damages in European jurisdictions.

items.title_zh: 快讯：英国高等法院就三星诉中兴案作出FRAND判决
items.title_en: UK High Court Issues First Global Cross-Licence FRAND Judgment in Samsung v ZTE
items.summary: The High Court has today handed down its judgment in Samsung v ZTE, determining the FRAND terms of a global cross-licence between the parties. This is t...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国高等法院就三星诉中兴案作出判决，确定双方全球交叉许可的FRAND条款。这是英国首例此类判决。法院详细指导了使用可比协议法确定FRAND条款的方法，特别是识别“良好可比协议”及调整非FRAND因素（如诉讼意愿和能力差异）。法院认为中兴因诉讼地位较弱而获得低价，现有权要求全额FRAND价值。判决主要基于中兴与苹果的可比许可协议。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK High Court has delivered its judgment in Samsung v ZTE, establishing the FRAND terms for a global cross-licence between the parties. This marks the first instance of such a determination reaching judgment in the United Kingdom. The decision offers detailed guidance on applying the comparable licences method, specifically regarding the identification of 'good comparables' and necessary adjustments for non-FRAND factors. The Court noted that ZTE’s perceived lower willingness or ability to litigate resulted in lower royalty rates in previous agreements with major licensees like Apple and Samsung. Consequently, the Court ruled that ZTE is entitled to full FRAND value, rejecting claims of hold-out by the licensors. The final determination relied primarily on the ZTE-Apple licence, which was deemed easier to unpack and adjust for differences in licensee status.
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies how courts may adjust FRAND rates based on disparities in litigation capability and willingness. Parties should proactively document evidence demonstrating their status as willing licensees to counter potential hold-out allegations. It is advisable to systematically review internal licensing agreements to quantify any historical disadvantages caused by unequal bargaining power or litigation resources. Such preparation helps ensure that future rate determinations reflect true market value rather than concessions made under duress. Monitoring how courts define and weigh these non-FRAND factors will be critical for developing robust negotiation strategies and defense positions in subsequent SEP disputes across European jurisdictions.

items.title: LD Mannheim, April 20, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1291/2026, Anti-Anti Suit Injunction Against “Interim Licence” Application Before Chinese Court
items.title_zh: 曼海姆地院：UPC有权签发针对中国法院临时许可的反反诉禁令
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Issues Anti-Anti-Suit Injunction Against Chinese Court Interim Licence
items.summary: The UPC has jurisdiction to issue an AASI to secure pending patent infringement proceedings before the UPC (Art. 31, 32(1)(c), 33(1)(a) UPCA) (mn. 16 et seq.)
items.ai_summary_zh: 曼海姆地方分部裁定，统一专利法院（UPC）依据《UPC协议》第31、32及33条，有权签发反反诉禁令（AASI）以保障待决侵权程序。法院认定，旨在阻止专利权人在UPC缔约国行使权利的外国禁令申请构成即将发生的侵权。中国法院签发的全球“临时许可”实质为反诉禁令，若被接受将违反国际民事诉讼基本原则。该裁决强化了UPC在跨国SEP纠纷中的管辖权与救济能力。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Local Division in Mannheim ruled that it possesses jurisdiction to issue an anti-anti-suit injunction (AASI) to protect pending patent infringement proceedings under Articles 31, 32(1)(c), and 33(1)(a) of the UPC Agreement. The Court determined that applying for a foreign injunction to prevent a patentee from enforcing rights within UPC Contracting States constitutes imminent infringement. Consequently, a worldwide 'Interim Licence' granted by a Chinese court was characterized as a de facto anti-suit injunction designed to block injunctive relief in foreign forums, including the UPC. This ruling reinforces the UPC’s authority to secure its judicial remedies against parallel foreign proceedings that undermine its jurisdiction in standard-essential patent disputes.
items.ai_insight_en: This decision signals that reliance on foreign rate-setting orders or interim licences to halt UPC litigation is increasingly ineffective. Parties involved in cross-border SEP disputes should reassess strategies that directly challenge UPC jurisdiction through parallel foreign proceedings. Instead, focusing on robust FRAND compliance defenses and engaging in good-faith settlement negotiations may offer more viable pathways to resolve conflicts. Ignoring this shift risks severe financial sanctions and potential market access restrictions due to non-compliance with UPC orders, necessitating a recalibration of global litigation tactics.

Practice note: Avoid direct jurisdictional confrontations via foreign courts; prioritize substantive FRAND arguments within the UPC framework.
What to monitor: Enforcement mechanisms for UPC AASIs and potential financial penalties for non-compliance with such orders.

items.title: LD Paris, April 10, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_301/2025
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：FRAND抗辩中原告享有最后陈述权
items.title_en: UPC Paris: Claimant Entitled to Final Submission in FRAND Defence Proceedings
items.summary: Unlike in case of a counterclaim for revocation (R. 29 et seq. RoP) or an application to amend the patent (R. 30 et seq. RoP), where the number and content of the pleadings are precisely set out, the Rules of Procedure do not contain respective provisions with respect to a FRAND defence.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎地方法院在Orange诉HMD案中发布程序令，指出《程序规则》未规定FRAND抗辩的具体答辩次数。法院依据公平与对抗原则，裁定作为FRAND抗辩反被告的原告有权进行最后陈述以确保公平审判，无需评估论点是否新颖。此举允许原告提交最终意见并驳回被告进一步回应的请求，填补了FRAND抗辩程序规则的空白。
items.ai_summary_en: In Orange SA v. HMD Global Oy, the UPC Local Division in Paris issued a procedural order addressing the absence of specific pleading rules for FRAND defences under the Rules of Procedure. Unlike provisions for revocation counterclaims or patent amendments, the Rules do not prescribe the number or content of pleadings for FRAND issues. Relying on general principles of fairness and adversarial proceedings from the Preamble, the Court held that the claimant, acting as counter-defendant to the FRAND defence, is entitled to the final word to ensure a fair trial. This ruling permits the claimant’s final submission while denying the defendant’s request for further response, without assessing whether the FRAND arguments were new. This decision clarifies procedural expectations and fills a gap in the current framework.
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling establishes a significant procedural dynamic where the party asserting infringement retains the final say on FRAND issues. For defendants raising FRAND defences, this creates a strategic imperative to present comprehensive arguments and evidence early in the proceedings. Reliance on subsequent rebuttals is no longer viable. Legal teams must prioritize thorough preparation for initial and intermediate stages to mitigate the disadvantage of being unable to respond to the claimant’s final submissions. Proactive case management is essential to lock in key factual and legal positions before the claimant exercises their right to the last word.

items.title_zh: UPC 上诉法院裁定初步异议可由合议庭决定并入主案审理
items.title_en: UPC CoA: Panel May Defer Preliminary Objections to Main Proceedings, Not Just Judge-Rapporteur
items.summary: The Court of Appeal clarifies that a decision under R. 20.2 RoP to deal with a preliminary objection in the main proceedings is not reserved to the judge-rapporteur alone.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉法院在涉及 FRAND 抗辩的专利侵权案中明确，依据程序规则第 20.2 条，将初步异议留待主案审理的决定权不仅属于独任法官，合议庭亦可行使。法院确认一审拥有广泛的案件管理裁量权，只要程序适当即可推迟裁决异议。本案中巴黎分部将可受理性问题与实体合并审理未越权，上诉审查范围因此受限。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal clarified that under Rule 20.2 of the Rules of Procedure, the decision to defer a preliminary objection to the main proceedings is not reserved exclusively to the judge-rapporteur. The panel itself may determine that the objection should be addressed alongside the merits. This ruling confirms that the Court of First Instance possesses broad case-management discretion to decide whether to rule on preliminary objections promptly or defer them, depending on procedural appropriateness. Consequently, appellate review of such case-management decisions is limited. In this specific FRAND-related patent dispute, the Paris Local Division was entitled to treat the admissibility issue as sufficiently linked to the infringement action and injunction request, allowing it to be resolved within the main proceedings rather than as a standalone preliminary matter.
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment underscores the significant case-management discretion afforded to UPC divisions, particularly regarding the timing of preliminary objection rulings. Parties facing SEP litigation must anticipate that tribunals may merge jurisdictional or validity challenges with substantive merits, especially where FRAND defenses are implicated. Litigants should prepare comprehensive strategies that address both procedural admissibility and substantive infringement simultaneously, rather than relying on early dismissal opportunities. Understanding how different local divisions exercise this discretion is critical for effective litigation planning and resource allocation in European patent disputes.

items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Replaces External-Eyes-Only With Licensing Bar For Defendants’ Employees
items.summary: In two separate decisions handed down on the same day, the Court of Appeal of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) imposed a licensing bar on...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院在 Sun v Vivo 及 Ericsson v ASUS 案中驳回原告“仅限外部专家”（EEO）的保密请求。法院认为完全排除被告员工访问保密许可协议可能损害其公平审判权，转而裁定对特定员工实施“许可禁令”，即禁止其在一定期限内参与第三方许可谈判。此举平衡了商业秘密保护与诉讼权利，确立了 UPC 处理 FRAND 案件中保密俱乐部的新标准。
items.ai_summary_en: In two separate decisions issued on the same day, the Unified Patent Court Court of Appeal rejected claimants' requests for an external-eyes-only confidentiality regime in standard essential patent disputes involving Sun Patent Trust v Vivo and Ericsson v ASUS. The Court prioritized defendants' right to effective remedies and fair trial access over broad exclusion of employees. Instead, it upheld a licensing bar prohibiting specific named employees from participating in third-party licensing negotiations for a defined period. This approach balances trade secret protection with procedural fairness, establishing a new precedent for handling confidential information in FRAND litigation at the UPC.
items.ai_insight_en: Litigants in European standard essential patent disputes should anticipate that courts will generally grant defendant employees access to core licensing data rather than imposing total isolation. Parties must proactively establish internal compliance mechanisms, including strict firewalls, to ensure designated personnel do not participate in relevant licensing negotiations. Violating these court-ordered bars may lead to adverse legal presumptions or sanctions. Consequently, strategic planning should focus on managing internal information flows and selecting appropriate non-negotiating staff for case coordination to mitigate regulatory risks while leveraging broader evidence disclosure opportunities.

items.summary: The US Department of Justice is investigating car manufacturers for participating in a licensee negotiation group (LNG), MLex has reported. The investig...
items.ai_summary_zh: 美国司法部正调查汽车制造商组成的被许可人谈判集团（ALNG），担忧其构成买方卡特尔违反谢尔曼法。此举与欧盟委员会及德国联邦卡特尔局的宽容立场形成鲜明对比，导致跨大西洋反垄断监管出现分歧。若无法消除美方顾虑，涉事车企可能面临诉讼，这为全球 SEP 许可谈判带来巨大不确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: The US Department of Justice is investigating major car manufacturers for participating in an automotive licensing negotiation group (ALNG), raising concerns that the initiative constitutes an illegal buyer cartel under Section 1 of the Sherman Act. This enforcement action underscores a significant transatlantic regulatory divergence, contrasting sharply with the European Commission’s recent comfort letter and guidance from the German Bundeskartellamt, which viewed the ALNG favorably under EU competition law. The DOJ’s hostile stance creates substantial legal uncertainty for global standard essential patent (SEP) licensing strategies. If the automakers cannot alleviate US antitrust concerns, they face potential federal litigation. This conflict complicates compliance for companies operating across both jurisdictions, as global SEP licenses must now navigate opposing regulatory expectations regarding collective bargaining by implementers.
items.ai_insight_en: Technology licensors and implementers must navigate increasing antitrust divergence between US and EU approaches to collective licensing. While EU guidelines increasingly accommodate licensee negotiation groups to reduce transaction costs, US enforcement remains skeptical of such arrangements as potential buyer cartels. Companies engaging in cross-border SEP licensing should establish robust compliance firewalls to isolate US operations from collective bargaining activities permitted or encouraged in Europe. It is critical to document that joint negotiations aim to improve efficiency and reduce information asymmetry rather than suppress royalty rates. Legal teams should prepare for heightened scrutiny in the US market, ensuring that any collaborative efforts among implementers are carefully structured to avoid allegations of coordinated hold-out or price-fixing under Sherman Act standards.

items.title: Can AI be sued?
items.title_zh: 英国司法任务组咨询：AI致损责任如何适用普通法？
items.title_en: UK Jurisdiction Taskforce Consults on Applying English Private Law to AI Harm
items.summary: The UK Jurisdiction Taskforce (UKJT) is consulting on a Legal Statement exploring how English private law might deal with harm caused by AI systems. Th...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国司法任务组（UKJT）正就法律声明进行咨询，探讨英格兰私法如何处理AI系统造成的损害。鉴于缺乏专门的AI责任制度，UKJT旨在澄清现有普通法原则如何适用于AI引发的新颖情况，以消除法律不确定性，促进技术创新。该声明不提议新法，而是解释法院如何适应性地应用现有法律，为科技界提供明确的风险指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Jurisdiction Taskforce (UKJT) is currently consulting on a Legal Statement that examines how English private law addresses harm caused by artificial intelligence systems. With no specific statutory liability regimes for AI in the United Kingdom, the UKJT aims to clarify how existing common law principles apply to these novel technological scenarios. The primary objective is to reduce legal uncertainty, which can otherwise hinder innovation and leave businesses unsure about their risk exposure. By explaining how courts are likely to adapt flexible and well-developed common law doctrines to autonomous systems, the statement seeks to provide greater certainty for the business and technology sectors. The final version will be published after considering stakeholder responses, focusing on interpretation rather than proposing new legislation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧AI企业需关注普通法对自主系统责任的解释趋势。虽无新法，但司法适应性可能带来不可预见的判例风险。建议企业完善内部合规与风险评估机制，积极参与咨询反馈，以影响最终法律立场的形成，确保业务模式符合潜在的责任认定标准。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating within or targeting the UK market, understanding the trajectory of common law adaptation regarding autonomous systems is critical. Although no new legislative framework is being introduced, the judicial interpretation of liability for AI-induced harm may evolve unpredictably. Organizations should proactively assess their internal compliance structures and risk management protocols against potential common law standards. The article highlights that if proposed amendments alter the subject matter of the dispute or cause significant delays, the court is likely to reject them to maintain procedural speed. This guidance offers critical strategic insights for parties involved in UPC litigation, emphasizing the importance of timing and procedural conduct when seeking or opposing amendments in urgent interim relief contexts.
items.ai_insight_en: Parties facing UPC preliminary injunctions should anticipate potential strategies where opponents seek patent amendments to circumvent invalidity defenses. items.title: UK judge Meade determines FRAND rate of $392m in Samsung vs ZTE
items.title_zh: 英国法官Meade裁定三星诉中兴FRAND许可费为3.92亿美元
items.title_en: UK High Court Sets $392m FRAND Rate in Samsung v ZTE Amid Global Jurisdictional Conflicts
items.summary: In an international dispute over a cross-licence for 4G and 5G patents, the UK High Court set a licence rate in Samsung vs ZTE.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国高等法院在三星与中兴的4G/5G专利交叉许可纠纷中，由Meade法官裁定全球FRAND许可费率为3.92亿美元。鉴于三星智能手机销量远高于中兴，其需作为净支付方。与此同时，德国慕尼黑地方法院授予中兴针对三星的禁令，且重庆一中院认定中兴报价符合FRAND原则。此案凸显了跨国SEP诉讼中不同法域裁判结果的冲突与博弈。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK High Court, presided over by Mr. Justice Meade, determined a global FRAND royalty rate of $392 million for the cross-licensing of 4G and 5G standard-essential patents between Samsung and ZTE. The judgment establishes Samsung as the net payer due to its significantly higher smartphone sales volume compared to ZTE. This ruling emerges amidst intense multi-jurisdictional litigation. Concurrently, the Munich Regional Court granted an injunction against Samsung while rejecting its FRAND defenses, and the Chongqing First Intermediate People’s Court ruled that ZTE’s licensing offer complied with FRAND principles. These divergent outcomes across the UK, Germany, and China highlight the strategic complexity and conflicting judicial approaches characterizing modern international SEP disputes.
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in Europe face heightened risks from concurrent 'injunction plus rate-setting' strategies across multiple jurisdictions. Litigants should adopt parallel proceedings to leverage favorable foreign judgments, such as Chinese FRAND determinations, to counterbalance European injunctive relief. Precise calculation of global sales ratios is critical to substantiating FRAND rate claims and avoiding disadvantageous pricing precedents in single markets. Strategic jurisdiction selection remains vital to mitigate enforcement risks and optimize licensing outcomes in complex cross-border patent portfolios.

items.title: UPC gives Frankfurt court priority in Samsung's FRAND claim but revokes ZTE's patent
items.title_zh: UPC曼海姆分部：驳回三星FRAND反诉，撤销中兴涉案专利
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Declines FRAND Jurisdiction for Samsung, Revokes ZTE Patent
items.summary: Mannheim local division has now revoked a patent owned by ZTE and declined jurisdiction over the FRAND counterclaim filed by Samsung.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分部在三星与中兴的平行诉讼中作出裁决。首先，法院依据《布鲁塞尔条例》第29条，认定法兰克福地方法院已受理的FRAND反诉具有优先管辖权，故驳回三星在UPC的同类申请，尽管双方实体形式不同但利益一致。其次，法院在侵权听证后撤销了中兴持有的EP 3 905 730号专利。此案例凸显了UPC在处理跨国FRAND许可费率设定时的程序限制及对专利有效性的严格审查。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Mannheim Local Division has issued significant rulings in the parallel litigation between ZTE and Samsung. First, the court declined jurisdiction over Samsung’s FRAND counterclaim, deferring to the Regional Court of Frankfurt due to pending parallel proceedings. Applying Article 29 of the Brussels Regulation, the UPC determined that despite involving formally different group entities, the parties shared identical interests, thereby prohibiting duplicate claims across EU courts. Second, following an infringement hearing regarding European Patent EP 3 905 730, the Mannheim division revoked the patent entirely. This decision underscores the UPC’s cautious approach to determining FRAND licensing rates when national courts are already seized of the matter. It also highlights the court’s willingness to conduct rigorous validity assessments during infringement proceedings, potentially altering the strategic landscape for SEP enforcement and defense in Europe.
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling illustrates the UPC’s procedural restraint regarding FRAND rate-setting when national courts have already assumed jurisdiction. Rights holders must carefully coordinate multi-jurisdictional strategies to avoid forum shopping pitfalls. The strict application of lis pendens rules to corporate groups signals that formal entity distinctions will not shield against jurisdictional challenges based on identical interests. Consequently, companies should prioritize robust patent validity analyses early in litigation. Relying solely on infringement actions without securing patent stability carries significant risk, as revocation can swiftly dismantle enforcement efforts. Monitoring how UPC divisions interact with national courts on FRAND issues remains critical for effective SEP portfolio management.

items.title: Regional Court Munich puts decision in ZTE vs Samsung on hold

items.title_en: Munich Regional Court Suspends ZTE v Samsung SEP Infringement Case Pending Validity Ruling
items.summary: The Regional Court Munich has handed down another decision in the complex dispute between ZTE and Samsung over SEPs.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院在 ZTE 与三星的 4G/5G 标准必要专利纠纷中，因联邦专利法院对涉案专利有效性存疑，决定中止侵权诉讼程序。该案原被视为检验慕尼黑法院不同审判庭对 FRAND 抗辩新立场的风向标，现需等待 2027 年无效审理结果，导致案件显著延期。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Regional Court has suspended infringement proceedings in the ongoing standard-essential patent dispute between ZTE and Samsung. The decision follows a preliminary opinion from the Federal Patent Court expressing doubts regarding the validity of ZTE’s patent EP 2 375 827. Consequently, the infringement suit is stayed until the nullity action is resolved, with hearings scheduled for mid-2027. This case was closely watched as a potential indicator of how different chambers within the Munich court would align on recent FRAND defense approaches established by another chamber. The suspension highlights the continued impact of Germany’s bifurcated system, where validity challenges can significantly delay infringement judgments. Both parties are engaged in eight parallel actions across the Unified Patent Court and German courts concerning 4G and 5G technologies.
items.ai_insight_en: In Germany’s bifurcated patent system, initiating parallel nullity proceedings remains a critical strategy for defendants facing SEP infringement claims. By challenging validity early, rights holders or alleged infringers can secure stays that prevent immediate injunctions, thereby buying time for licensing negotiations or technical workarounds. However, stakeholders must anticipate prolonged uncertainty and increased legal costs associated with extended timelines. Effective management requires robust financial planning and proactive engagement in FRAND discussions during these delays. Monitoring how different judicial chambers interpret FRAND obligations alongside validity challenges will be essential for predicting case trajectories and managing portfolio risks effectively.

items.title: On the way to a global FRAND rate: Nokia drops claims against Warner and Paramount
items.title_zh: 迈向全球FRAND费率：诺基亚撤回对华纳和派拉蒙起诉
items.title_en: Nokia Withdraws UPC and Munich Actions After UK Global FRAND Rate Agreement
items.summary: After agreeing to UK FRAND rate setting, Nokia has withdrawn its pending actions against Warner Bros. and Paramount at the UPC and in Munich.
items.ai_summary_zh: 在同意英国设定全球RAND费率后，诺基亚撤回了在统一专利法院（UPC）及慕尼黑地方法院针对华纳兄弟和派拉蒙 pending 的诉讼。UPC登记显示，涉及EP 4 250 732专利的案件已撤诉；慕尼黑法院确认涉及EP 4 099 700和EP 2 130 150的案件亦被撤回。此举表明通过单一法域确定全球许可费率正成为解决跨国SEP纠纷的高效路径，显著改变了传统的平行诉讼格局。
items.ai_summary_en: Following an agreement to establish a global RAND rate in the United Kingdom, Nokia has withdrawn its pending infringement actions against Warner Bros. and Paramount at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and the Munich Regional Court. According to the UPC register, proceedings concerning patent EP 4 250 732 were discontinued before Easter. The Munich court confirmed that claims regarding patents EP 4 099 700 and EP 2 130 150 were also withdrawn. These developments highlight the growing influence of UK courts in determining global licensing rates for standard-essential patents. This trend is reshaping traditional parallel litigation strategies, encouraging parties to resolve cross-border disputes through single-jurisdiction rate-setting rather than pursuing fragmented enforcement actions across multiple European venues.
items.ai_insight_en: The withdrawal of parallel proceedings following a UK global rate decision underscores the strategic value of English jurisdiction in SEP disputes. Rights holders increasingly view single-jurisdiction determinations as efficient mechanisms to secure comprehensive licensing terms, reducing the need for costly multi-forum litigation. For implementers, this shift suggests that engaging in UK rate-setting proceedings can serve as a powerful lever to achieve global settlements and mitigate injunction risks in Europe. Legal teams should assess whether participating in UK determinations offers a more predictable and cost-effective path to resolving multinational SEP conflicts compared to defending against separate actions in the UPC or national courts.

items.title: Local division Mannheim flirts with setting FRAND rate
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆分部倾向裁定 FRAND 费率
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Division Signals Openness to Determining FRAND Rates in ZTE v Samsung
items.summary: Following Munich Regional Court, Mannheim local division is now also opening up to making a FRAND-rate determination.
items.ai_summary_zh: 继慕尼黑地方法院后，统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院在 ZTE 诉三星案中展现新动向。主审法官托赫特曼提出 UPC 框架下裁定 FRAND 费率的程序构想，打破德国法院长期回避此议题的传统。因三星提出反诉，法庭需对此作出裁决，预示欧洲标准必要专利诉讼格局生变。
items.ai_summary_en: Following the Munich Regional Court, the Unified Patent Court’s Mannheim Local Division is shifting away from the traditional German judicial reluctance to determine Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) royalty rates. In the ongoing ZTE v Samsung proceedings, Presiding Judge Peter Tochtermann outlined a procedural framework for such determinations within the UPC system. This development arises because Samsung filed a FRAND counterclaim, compelling the court to address the issue substantively rather than avoiding it. The hearing attracted significant attention from legal and economic experts across Europe and Asia. This move suggests a potential transformation in European standard-essential patent litigation, challenging the historical dominance of UK courts in setting global FRAND terms and establishing new precedents for continental European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in standard-essential patent disputes should anticipate increased judicial willingness in Germany to set binding FRAND rates.
